I've uploaded a code repository in the directory /testbed. Consider the following PR description:

<pr_description>
[Feature]: zeroclaw props CLI for config property management
**Affected component:** runtime/daemon
**Severity:** S2 - degraded behavior

## Problem statement

There is no CLI command to manage individual config properties or set
encrypted secrets in config.toml. Users must either re-run the full
`zeroclaw onboard` wizard or manually edit config.toml.

## Solution (PR #5165)

Evolved from a secret-only CLI into a general-purpose config property
management system:

### Configurable derive macro (`crates/zeroclaw-macros/`)

- `#[derive(Configurable)]` with `#[secret]`, `#[nested]`, `#[prefix]` attributes
- Generates `prop_fields()`, `get_prop()`, `set_prop()`, `prop_is_secret()`,
  `init_defaults()`, `secret_fields()`, `encrypt_secrets()`, `decrypt_secrets()`
- `get_prop`/`set_prop` use serde roundtrip — macro generates only metadata,
  not per-field type dispatch
- `HasPropKind` trait maps types to `PropKind` at compile time; scalars in
  `traits.rs`, config enums via `impl_enum_prop_kind!`
- Handles `HashMap<String, T>` with `#[nested]` and `Vec<String>` with `#[secret]`
- All ~80 config structs annotated — every property individually addressable

### `zeroclaw props` CLI

- `props list [--filter PREFIX] [--secrets]` — enumerate all properties with values, types, categories
- `props get <path>` — get a value (secrets show set/unset status only)
- `props set <path> [value]` — set a property; secrets auto-detect masked input; enums offer interactive `dialoguer::Select`
- `props init [section]` — instantiate unconfigured sections with defaults (`enabled=false`)
- `props complete [partial]` — dynamic shell tab-completion for property paths

### Additional changes

- `enabled: bool` field on all channel configs for explicit activation
- `backfill_enabled()` auto-enables channels present in config.toml without explicit `enabled` key
- Shell completion hooks (bash/zsh/fish) wrap clap's generated function
- Manual encrypt/decrypt helpers replaced by macro-derived methods
- Exhaustive walk tests verify every prop and every enum variant

## Future work

- **`api_key_env` pattern replacement**: Several configs (Firecrawl, LinkedIn image providers, ImageGen) store env var names (`api_key_env: "STABILITY_API_KEY"`) instead of actual secret values. These should be replaced with `#[secret] api_key` fields that store the key directly (encrypted at rest), with env var fallback handled at runtime. This prevents users from accidentally putting actual keys in the env-var-name field.
- **`props unset`**: Dedicated subcommand to clear Option fields (currently works via `props set path ""` but not discoverable)
- **`props get` for secrets**: Decrypt and display secret values (with confirmation prompt)
- **Vec/HashMap field support in props**: Currently skipped — users can't set list or map fields via props
- **User-facing docs**: `docs/cli/props.md` reference page for the props subcommand

Part of umbrella tracker #4657.

## Non-goals

- Not a general key-value store
- Not a replacement for `zeroclaw onboard`

## Breaking change?

No — existing encrypted secrets continue to work. New `enabled` field
defaults to `false`; `backfill_enabled()` preserves existing channel
activation.

---

**Data hygiene checks:**
- [x] Removed personal/sensitive data from examples
- [x] Used neutral, project-focused wording

</pr_description>

    63	/// Top-level ZeroClaw configuration, loaded from `config.toml`.
    64	///
    65	/// Resolution order: `ZEROCLAW_WORKSPACE` env → `active_workspace.toml` marker → `~/.zeroclaw/config.toml`.
    66	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema)]
    67	pub struct Config {
    68	    /// Workspace directory - computed from home, not serialized
    69	    #[serde(skip)]
    70	    pub workspace_dir: PathBuf,
    71	    /// Path to config.toml - computed from home, not serialized
    72	    #[serde(skip)]
    73	    pub config_path: PathBuf,
    74	    /// API key for the selected provider. Overridden by `ZEROCLAW_API_KEY` or `API_KEY` env vars.
    75	    pub api_key: Option<String>,
    76	    /// Base URL override for provider API (e.g. "http://10.0.0.1:11434" for remote Ollama)
    77	    pub api_url: Option<String>,
    78	    /// Custom API path suffix for OpenAI-compatible / custom providers
    79	    /// (e.g. "/v2/generate" instead of the default "/v1/chat/completions").
    80	    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    81	    pub api_path: Option<String>,
    82	    /// Default provider ID or alias (e.g. `"openrouter"`, `"ollama"`, `"anthropic"`). Default: `"openrouter"`.
    83	    #[serde(alias = "model_provider")]
    84	    pub default_provider: Option<String>,
    85	    /// Default model routed through the selected provider (e.g. `"anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"`).
    86	    #[serde(alias = "model")]
    87	    pub default_model: Option<String>,
    88	    /// Optional named provider profiles keyed by id (Codex app-server compatible layout).
    89	    #[serde(default)]
    90	    pub model_providers: HashMap<String, ModelProviderConfig>,
    91	    /// Default model temperature (0.0–2.0). Default: `0.7`.
    92	    #[serde(

    94	        deserialize_with = "deserialize_temperature"
    95	    )]
    96	    pub default_temperature: f64,

    99	    ///
   100	    /// Increase for slower backends (e.g., llama.cpp on constrained hardware)
   101	    /// that need more time processing large contexts.

   106	    ///
   107	    /// When set, overrides each provider's built-in default. This is especially
   108	    /// important for OpenRouter where the platform default (65536) can cause 402
   109	    /// errors for models with lower output limits.
   110	    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]

   117	    /// augment (and override) the program's default headers.
   118	    ///
   119	    /// Can also be set via `ZEROCLAW_EXTRA_HEADERS` environment variable using

   249	    /// Browser delegation configuration (`[browser_delegate]`).
   250	    ///
   251	    /// Delegates browser-based tasks to a browser-capable CLI subprocess (e.g.
   252	    /// Claude Code with `claude-in-chrome` MCP tools). Useful for interacting
   253	    /// with corporate web apps (Teams, Outlook, Jira, Confluence) that lack
   254	    /// direct API access. A persistent Chrome profile can be configured so SSO
   255	    /// sessions survive across invocations.
   256	    ///
   257	    /// Fields:
   258	    /// - `enabled` (`bool`, default `false`) — enable the browser delegation tool.
   259	    /// - `cli_binary` (`String`, default `"claude"`) — CLI binary to spawn for browser tasks.
   260	    /// - `chrome_profile_dir` (`String`, default `""`) — Chrome user-data directory for
   261	    ///   persistent SSO sessions. When empty, a fresh profile is used each invocation.
   262	    /// - `allowed_domains` (`Vec<String>`, default `[]`) — allowlist of domains the browser
   263	    ///   may navigate to. Empty means all non-blocked domains are permitted.
   264	    /// - `blocked_domains` (`Vec<String>`, default `[]`) — denylist of domains. Blocked
   265	    ///   domains take precedence over allowed domains.
   266	    /// - `task_timeout_secs` (`u64`, default `120`) — per-task timeout in seconds.
   267	    ///
   268	    /// Compatibility: additive and disabled by default; existing configs remain valid when omitted.
   269	    /// Rollback/migration: remove `[browser_delegate]` or keep `enabled = false` to disable.
   270	    #[serde(default)]
   271	    pub browser_delegate: crate::tools::browser_delegate::BrowserDelegateConfig,

    45	fn parse_temperature(s: &str) -> std::result::Result<f64, String> {
    46	    let t: f64 = s.parse().map_err(|e| format!("{e}"))?;
    47	    config::schema::validate_temperature(t)
    48	}

    50	fn print_no_command_help() -> Result<()> {
    51	    println!("No command provided.");
    52	    println!("Try `zeroclaw onboard` to initialize your workspace.");
    53	    println!();
    54	
    55	    let mut cmd = Cli::command();
    56	    cmd.print_help()?;
    57	    println!();
    58	
    59	    #[cfg(windows)]
    60	    pause_after_no_command_help();
    61	
    62	    Ok(())
    63	}
    64	
    65	#[cfg(windows)]
    66	fn pause_after_no_command_help() {
    67	    println!();
    68	    print!("Press Enter to exit...");
    69	    let _ = std::io::stdout().flush();
    70	    let mut line = String::new();
    71	    let _ = std::io::stdin().read_line(&mut line);
    72	}

   152	/// `ZeroClaw` - Zero overhead. Zero compromise. 100% Rust.
   153	#[derive(Parser, Debug)]

   157	#[command(about = "The fastest, smallest AI assistant.", long_about = None)]
   158	struct Cli {
   159	    #[arg(long, global = true)]

   167	enum Commands {
   168	    /// Initialize your workspace and configuration
   169	    Onboard {

   192	        /// Memory backend (sqlite, lucid, markdown, none) - used in quick mode, default: sqlite
   193	        #[arg(long)]

   824	#[tokio::main]
   825	#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]
   826	async fn main() -> Result<()> {
   827	    // Install default crypto provider for Rustls TLS.
   828	    // This prevents the error: "could not automatically determine the process-level CryptoProvider"
   829	    // when both aws-lc-rs and ring features are available (or neither is explicitly selected).
   830	    if let Err(e) = rustls::crypto::ring::default_provider().install_default() {
   831	        eprintln!("Warning: Failed to install default crypto provider: {e:?}");
   832	    }
   833	
   834	    if std::env::args_os().len() <= 1 {
   835	        return print_no_command_help();
   836	    }
   837	
   838	    let cli = Cli::parse();
   839	
   840	    if let Some(config_dir) = &cli.config_dir {
   841	        if config_dir.trim().is_empty() {
   842	            bail!("--config-dir cannot be empty");
   843	        }
   844	        // SAFETY: called early in main before any threads are spawned.
   845	        unsafe { std::env::set_var("ZEROCLAW_CONFIG_DIR", config_dir) };
   846	    }
   847	
   848	    // Completions must remain stdout-only and should not load config or initialize logging.
   849	    // This avoids warnings/log lines corrupting sourced completion scripts.
   850	    if let Commands::Completions { shell } = &cli.command {
   851	        let mut stdout = std::io::stdout().lock();
   852	        write_shell_completion(*shell, &mut stdout)?;
   853	        return Ok(());
   854	    }
   855	
   856	    // Initialize logging - respects RUST_LOG env var, defaults to INFO
   857	    let subscriber = fmt::Subscriber::builder()
   858	        .with_env_filter(
   859	            EnvFilter::try_from_default_env().unwrap_or_else(|_| EnvFilter::new("info")),
   860	        )
   861	        .finish();
   862	
   863	    tracing::subscriber::set_global_default(subscriber).expect("setting default subscriber failed");
   864	
   865	    // Onboard auto-detects the environment: if stdin/stdout are a TTY and no
   866	    // provider flags were given, it runs the full interactive wizard; otherwise
   867	    // it runs the quick (scriptable) setup.  Use --quick to force quick setup,
   868	    // or set ZEROCLAW_INTERACTIVE=1 to force interactive mode when TTY
   869	    // detection fails.  This means `curl … | bash` and
   870	    // `zeroclaw onboard --api-key …` both take the fast path, while a bare
   871	    // `zeroclaw onboard` in a terminal launches the wizard.
   872	    if let Commands::Onboard {
   873	        force,
   874	        reinit,
   875	        channels_only,
   876	        api_key,
   877	        provider,
   878	        model,
   879	        memory,
   880	        quick,
   881	        tui: use_tui,
   882	    } = &cli.command
   883	    {
   884	        let force = *force;
   885	        let reinit = *reinit;
   886	        let channels_only = *channels_only;
   887	        let api_key = api_key.clone();
   888	        let provider = provider.clone();
   889	        let model = model.clone();
   890	        let memory = memory.clone();
   891	        let quick = *quick;
   892	        let use_tui = *use_tui;
   893	
   894	        if reinit && channels_only {
   895	            bail!("--reinit and --channels-only cannot be used together");
   896	        }
   897	        if channels_only
   898	            && (api_key.is_some() || provider.is_some() || model.is_some() || memory.is_some())
   899	        {
   900	            bail!("--channels-only does not accept --api-key, --provider, --model, or --memory");
   901	        }
   902	        if channels_only && force {
   903	            bail!("--channels-only does not accept --force");
   904	        }
   905	        if quick && channels_only {
   906	            bail!("--quick and --channels-only cannot be used together");
   907	        }
   908	
   909	        // Handle --reinit: backup and reset configuration
   910	        if reinit {
   911	            let (zeroclaw_dir, _) =
   912	                crate::config::schema::resolve_runtime_dirs_for_onboarding().await?;
   913	
   914	            if zeroclaw_dir.exists() {
   915	                let timestamp = chrono::Local::now().format("%Y%m%d%H%M%S");
   916	                let backup_dir = format!("{}.backup.{}", zeroclaw_dir.display(), timestamp);
   917	
   918	                println!("⚠️  Reinitializing ZeroClaw configuration...");
   919	                println!("   Current config directory: {}", zeroclaw_dir.display());
   920	                println!(
   921	                    "   This will back up your existing config to: {}",
   922	                    backup_dir
   923	                );
   924	                println!();
   925	                print!("Continue? [y/N] ");
   926	                std::io::stdout()
   927	                    .flush()
   928	                    .context("Failed to flush stdout")?;
   929	
   930	                let mut answer = String::new();
   931	                std::io::stdin().read_line(&mut answer)?;
   932	                if !answer.trim().eq_ignore_ascii_case("y") {
   933	                    println!("Aborted.");
   934	                    return Ok(());
   935	                }
   936	                println!();
   937	
   938	                // Rename existing directory as backup
   939	                tokio::fs::rename(&zeroclaw_dir, &backup_dir)
   940	                    .await
   941	                    .with_context(|| {
   942	                        format!("Failed to backup existing config to {}", backup_dir)
   943	                    })?;
   944	
   945	                println!("   Backup created successfully.");
   946	                println!("   Starting fresh initialization...\n");
   947	            }
   948	        }
   949	
   950	        // Auto-detect: run the interactive wizard when in a TTY with no
   951	        // provider flags, quick setup otherwise (scriptable path).
   952	        let has_provider_flags =
   953	            api_key.is_some() || provider.is_some() || model.is_some() || memory.is_some();
   954	        let is_tty = std::io::stdin().is_terminal() && std::io::stdout().is_terminal();
   955	        let env_interactive = std::env::var("ZEROCLAW_INTERACTIVE").as_deref() == Ok("1");
   956	
   957	        // TUI onboarding mode (ratatui-based)
   958	        if use_tui {
   959	            Box::pin(tui::run_tui_onboarding()).await?;
   960	            return Ok(());
   961	        }
   962	
   963	        let config = if channels_only {
   964	            Box::pin(onboard::run_channels_repair_wizard()).await
   965	        } else if quick || has_provider_flags {
   966	            Box::pin(onboard::run_quick_setup(
   967	                api_key.as_deref(),
   968	                provider.as_deref(),
   969	                model.as_deref(),
   970	                memory.as_deref(),
   971	                force,
   972	            ))
   973	            .await
   974	        } else if is_tty || env_interactive {
   975	            Box::pin(onboard::run_wizard(force)).await
   976	        } else {
   977	            Box::pin(onboard::run_quick_setup(
   978	                api_key.as_deref(),
   979	                provider.as_deref(),
   980	                model.as_deref(),
   981	                memory.as_deref(),
   982	                force,
   983	            ))
   984	            .await
   985	        }?;
   986	
   987	        if config.gateway.require_pairing {
   988	            println!();
   989	            println!("  Pairing is enabled. A one-time pairing code will be");
   990	            println!("  displayed when the gateway starts.");
   991	            println!("  Dashboard: http://127.0.0.1:{}", config.gateway.port);
   992	            println!();
   993	        }
   994	
   995	        // Auto-start channels if user said yes during wizard
   996	        if std::env::var("ZEROCLAW_AUTOSTART_CHANNELS").as_deref() == Ok("1") {
   997	            Box::pin(channels::start_channels(config)).await?;
   998	        }
   999	        return Ok(());
  1000	    }
  1001	
  1002	    // All other commands need config loaded first
  1003	    let mut config = Box::pin(Config::load_or_init()).await?;
  1004	    config.apply_env_overrides();
  1005	    observability::runtime_trace::init_from_config(&config.observability, &config.workspace_dir);
  1006	    if config.security.otp.enabled {
  1007	        let config_dir = config
  1008	            .config_path
  1009	            .parent()
  1010	            .context("Config path must have a parent directory")?;
  1011	        let store = security::SecretStore::new(config_dir, config.secrets.encrypt);
  1012	        let (_validator, enrollment_uri) =
  1013	            security::OtpValidator::from_config(&config.security.otp, config_dir, &store)?;
  1014	        if let Some(uri) = enrollment_uri {
  1015	            println!("Initialized OTP secret for ZeroClaw.");
  1016	            println!("Enrollment URI: {uri}");
  1017	        }
  1018	    }
  1019	
  1020	    match cli.command {
  1021	        Commands::Onboard { .. } | Commands::Completions { .. } => unreachable!(),
  1022	
  1023	        Commands::Agent {
  1024	            message,
  1025	            session_state_file,
  1026	            provider,
  1027	            model,
  1028	            temperature,
  1029	            peripheral,
  1030	        } => {
  1031	            let final_temperature = temperature.unwrap_or(config.default_temperature);
  1032	
  1033	            Box::pin(agent::run(
  1034	                config,
  1035	                message,
  1036	                provider,
  1037	                model,
  1038	                final_temperature,
  1039	                peripheral,
  1040	                true,
  1041	                session_state_file,
  1042	                None,
  1043	            ))
  1044	            .await
  1045	            .map(|_| ())
  1046	        }
  1047	
  1048	        Commands::Acp {
  1049	            max_sessions,
  1050	            session_timeout,
  1051	        } => {
  1052	            let mut acp_config = channels::acp_server::AcpServerConfig::default();
  1053	            if let Some(max) = max_sessions {
  1054	                acp_config.max_sessions = max;
  1055	            }
  1056	            if let Some(timeout) = session_timeout {
  1057	                acp_config.session_timeout_secs = timeout;
  1058	            }
  1059	            let server = channels::acp_server::AcpServer::new(config, acp_config);
  1060	            server.run().await
  1061	        }
  1062	
  1063	        Commands::Gateway { gateway_command } => {
  1064	            match gateway_command {
  1065	                Some(zeroclaw::GatewayCommands::Restart { port, host }) => {
  1066	                    let (port, host) = resolve_gateway_addr(&config, port, host);
  1067	                    let addr = format!("{host}:{port}");
  1068	                    info!("🔄 Restarting ZeroClaw Gateway on {addr}");
  1069	
  1070	                    // Try to gracefully shutdown existing gateway via admin endpoint
  1071	                    match shutdown_gateway(&host, port).await {
  1072	                        Ok(()) => {
  1073	                            info!("   ✓ Existing gateway on {addr} shut down gracefully");
  1074	                            // Poll until the port is free (connection refused) or timeout
  1075	                            let deadline =
  1076	                                tokio::time::Instant::now() + tokio::time::Duration::from_secs(5);
  1077	                            loop {
  1078	                                match tokio::net::TcpStream::connect(&addr).await {
  1079	                                    Err(_) => break, // port is free
  1080	                                    Ok(_) if tokio::time::Instant::now() >= deadline => {
  1081	                                        warn!(
  1082	                                            "   Timed out waiting for port {port} to be released"
  1083	                                        );
  1084	                                        break;
  1085	                                    }
  1086	                                    Ok(_) => {
  1087	                                        tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(50))
  1088	                                            .await;
  1089	                                    }
  1090	                                }
  1091	                            }
  1092	                        }
  1093	                        Err(e) => {
  1094	                            info!("   No existing gateway to shut down: {e}");
  1095	                        }
  1096	                    }
  1097	
  1098	                    log_gateway_start(&host, port);
  1099	                    Box::pin(gateway::run_gateway(&host, port, config, None)).await
  1100	                }
  1101	                Some(zeroclaw::GatewayCommands::GetPaircode { new }) => {
  1102	                    let port = config.gateway.port;
  1103	                    let host = &config.gateway.host;
  1104	
  1105	                    // Fetch live pairing code from running gateway
  1106	                    // If --new is specified, generate a fresh pairing code
  1107	                    match fetch_paircode(host, port, new).await {
  1108	                        Ok(Some(code)) => {
  1109	                            println!("🔐 Gateway pairing is enabled.");
  1110	                            println!();
  1111	                            println!("  ┌──────────────┐");
  1112	                            println!("  │  {code}  │");
  1113	                            println!("  └──────────────┘");
  1114	                            println!();
  1115	                            println!("  Use this one-time code to pair a new device:");
  1116	                            println!("    POST /pair with header X-Pairing-Code: {code}");
  1117	                        }
  1118	                        Ok(None) => {
  1119	                            if config.gateway.require_pairing {
  1120	                                println!(
  1121	                                    "🔐 Gateway pairing is enabled, but no active pairing code available."
  1122	                                );
  1123	                                println!(
  1124	                                    "   The gateway may already be paired, or the code has been used."
  1125	                                );
  1126	                                println!("   Restart the gateway to generate a new pairing code.");
  1127	                            } else {
  1128	                                println!("⚠️  Gateway pairing is disabled in config.");
  1129	                                println!(
  1130	                                    "   All requests will be accepted without authentication."
  1131	                                );
  1132	                                println!(
  1133	                                    "   To enable pairing, set [gateway] require_pairing = true"
  1134	                                );
  1135	                            }
  1136	                        }
  1137	                        Err(e) => {
  1138	                            println!(
  1139	                                "❌ Failed to fetch pairing code from gateway at {host}:{port}"
  1140	                            );
  1141	                            println!("   Error: {e}");
  1142	                            println!();
  1143	                            println!("   Is the gateway running? Start it with:");
  1144	                            println!("     zeroclaw gateway start");
  1145	                        }
  1146	                    }
  1147	                    Ok(())
  1148	                }
  1149	                Some(zeroclaw::GatewayCommands::Start { port, host }) => {
  1150	                    let (port, host) = resolve_gateway_addr(&config, port, host);
  1151	                    log_gateway_start(&host, port);
  1152	                    Box::pin(gateway::run_gateway(&host, port, config, None)).await
  1153	                }
  1154	                None => {
  1155	                    let port = config.gateway.port;
  1156	                    let host = config.gateway.host.clone();
  1157	                    log_gateway_start(&host, port);
  1158	                    Box::pin(gateway::run_gateway(&host, port, config, None)).await
  1159	                }
  1160	            }
  1161	        }
  1162	
  1163	        Commands::Daemon { port, host } => {
  1164	            if let Ok(exe) = std::env::current_exe() {
  1165	                let exe_str = exe.to_string_lossy();
  1166	                if exe_str.contains(".cargo/bin") || exe_str.contains("/home/") {
  1167	                    tracing::warn!(
  1168	                        "Daemon running from user home directory: {}. \
  1169	                         Consider installing to /usr/local/bin for system-wide service.",
  1170	                        exe_str
  1171	                    );
  1172	                }
  1173	            }
  1174	            let port = port.unwrap_or(config.gateway.port);
  1175	            let host = host.unwrap_or_else(|| config.gateway.host.clone());
  1176	            if port == 0 {
  1177	                info!("🧠 Starting ZeroClaw Daemon on {host} (random port)");
  1178	            } else {
  1179	                info!("🧠 Starting ZeroClaw Daemon on {host}:{port}");
  1180	            }
  1181	            Box::pin(daemon::run(config, host, port)).await
  1182	        }
  1183	
  1184	        Commands::Status { format } => {
  1185	            if format.as_deref() == Some("exit-code") {
  1186	                // Lightweight health probe for Docker HEALT<response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you.     88	/// Gateway management subcommands
    89	#[derive(Subcommand, Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
    90	pub enum GatewayCommands {
    91	    /// Start the gateway server (default if no subcommand specified)
    92	    #[command(long_about = "\
    93	Start the gateway server (webhooks, websockets).
    94	
    95	Runs the HTTP/WebSocket gateway that accepts incoming webhook events \
    96	and WebSocket connections. Bind address defaults to the values in \
    97	your config file (gateway.host / gateway.port).
    98	
    99	Examples:
   100	  zeroclaw gateway start              # use config defaults
   101	  zeroclaw gateway start -p 8080      # listen on port 8080
   102	  zeroclaw gateway start --host 0.0.0.0   # requires [gateway].allow_public_bind=true or a tunnel
   103	  zeroclaw gateway start -p 0         # random available port")]
   104	    Start {
   105	        /// Port to listen on (use 0 for random available port); defaults to config gateway.port
   106	        #[arg(short, long)]
   107	        port: Option<u16>,
   108	
   109	        /// Host to bind to; defaults to config gateway.host
   110	        /// Note: Binding to 0.0.0.0 requires `gateway.allow_public_bind = true` in config
   111	        #[arg(long)]
   112	        host: Option<String>,
   113	    },
   114	    /// Restart the gateway server
   115	    #[command(long_about = "\
   116	Restart the gateway server.
   117	
   118	Stops the running gateway if present, then starts a new instance \
   119	with the current configuration.
   120	
   121	Examples:
   122	  zeroclaw gateway restart            # restart with config defaults
   123	  zeroclaw gateway restart -p 8080    # restart on port 8080")]
   124	    Restart {
   125	        /// Port to listen on (use 0 for random available port); defaults to config gateway.port
   126	        #[arg(short, long)]
   127	        port: Option<u16>,
   128	
   129	        /// Host to bind to; defaults to config gateway.host
   130	        /// Note: Binding to 0.0.0.0 requires `gateway.allow_public_bind = true` in config
   131	        #[arg(long)]
   132	        host: Option<String>,
   133	    },
   134	    /// Show or generate the pairing code without restarting
   135	    #[command(long_about = "\
   136	Show or generate the gateway pairing code.
   137	
   138	Displays the pairing code for connecting new clients without \
   139	restarting the gateway. Requires the gateway to be running.
   140	
   141	With --new, generates a fresh pairing code even if the gateway \
   142	was previously paired (useful for adding additional clients).
   143	
   144	Examples:
   145	  zeroclaw gateway get-paircode       # show current pairing code
   146	  zeroclaw gateway get-paircode --new # generate a new pairing code")]
   147	    GetPaircode {
   148	        /// Generate a new pairing code (even if already paired)
   149	        #[arg(long)]
   150	        new: bool,
   151	    },
   152	}

   180	/// Channel management subcommands
   181	#[derive(Subcommand, Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
   182	pub enum ChannelCommands {
   183	    /// List all configured channels
   184	    List,
   185	    /// Start all configured channels (handled in main.rs for async)
   186	    Start,
   187	    /// Run health checks for configured channels (handled in main.rs for async)
   188	    Doctor,
   189	    /// Add a new channel configuration
   190	    #[command(long_about = "\
   191	Add a new channel configuration.
   192	
   193	Provide the channel type and a JSON object with the required \
   194	configuration keys for that channel type.
   195	
   196	Supported types: telegram, discord, slack, whatsapp, matrix, imessage, email.
   197	
   198	Examples:
   199	  zeroclaw channel add telegram '{\"bot_token\":\"...\",\"name\":\"my-bot\"}'
   200	  zeroclaw channel add discord '{\"bot_token\":\"...\",\"name\":\"my-discord\"}'")]
   201	    Add {
   202	        /// Channel type (telegram, discord, slack, whatsapp, matrix, imessage, email)
   203	        channel_type: String,
   204	        /// Optional configuration as JSON
   205	        config: String,
   206	    },
   207	    /// Remove a channel configuration
   208	    Remove {
   209	        /// Channel name to remove
   210	        name: String,
   211	    },
   212	    /// Bind a Telegram identity (username or numeric user ID) into allowlist
   213	    #[command(long_about = "\
   214	Bind a Telegram identity into the allowlist.
   215	
   216	Adds a Telegram username (without the '@' prefix) or numeric user \
   217	ID to the channel allowlist so the agent will respond to messages \
   218	from that identity.
   219	
   220	Examples:
   221	  zeroclaw channel bind-telegram zeroclaw_user
   222	  zeroclaw channel bind-telegram 123456789")]
   223	    BindTelegram {
   224	        /// Telegram identity to allow (username without '@' or numeric user ID)
   225	        identity: String,
   226	    },
   227	    /// Send a message to a configured channel
   228	    #[command(long_about = "\
   229	Send a one-off message to a configured channel.
   230	
   231	Sends a text message through the specified channel without starting \
   232	the full agent loop. Useful for scripted notifications, hardware \
   233	sensor alerts, and automation pipelines.
   234	
   235	The --channel-id selects the channel by its config section name \
   236	(e.g. 'telegram', 'discord', 'slack'). The --recipient is the \
   237	platform-specific destination (e.g. a Telegram chat ID).
   238	
   239	Examples:
   240	  zeroclaw channel send 'Someone is near your device.' --channel-id telegram --recipient 123456789
   241	  zeroclaw channel send 'Build succeeded!' --channel-id discord --recipient 987654321")]
   242	    Send {
   243	        /// Message text to send
   244	        message: String,
   245	        /// Channel config name (e.g. telegram, discord, slack)
   246	        #[arg(long)]
   247	        channel_id: String,
   248	        /// Recipient identifier (platform-specific, e.g. Telegram chat ID)
   249	        #[arg(long)]
   250	        recipient: String,
   251	    },
   252	}

   299	/// Cron subcommands
   300	#[derive(Subcommand, Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
   301	pub enum CronCommands {
   302	    /// List all scheduled tasks
   303	    List,
   304	    /// Add a new scheduled task
   305	    #[command(long_about = "\
   306	Add a new recurring scheduled task.
   307	
   308	Uses standard 5-field cron syntax: 'min hour day month weekday'. \
   309	Times are evaluated in UTC by default; use --tz with an IANA \
   310	timezone name to override.
   311	
   312	Examples:
   313	  zeroclaw cron add '0 9 * * 1-5' 'Good morning' --tz America/New_York --agent
   314	  zeroclaw cron add '*/30 * * * *' 'Check system health' --agent
   315	  zeroclaw cron add '*/5 * * * *' 'echo ok'")]
   316	    Add {
   317	        /// Cron expression
   318	        expression: String,
   319	        /// Optional IANA timezone (e.g. America/Los_Angeles)
   320	        #[arg(long)]
   321	        tz: Option<String>,
   322	        /// Treat the argument as an agent prompt instead of a shell command
   323	        #[arg(long)]
   324	        agent: bool,
   325	        /// Restrict agent cron jobs to the specified tool names (repeatable, agent-only)
   326	        #[arg(long = "allowed-tool")]
   327	        allowed_tools: Vec<String>,
   328	        /// Command (shell) or prompt (agent) to run
   329	        command: String,
   330	    },
   331	    /// Add a one-shot scheduled task at an RFC3339 timestamp
   332	    #[command(long_about = "\
   333	Add a one-shot task that fires at a specific UTC timestamp.
   334	
   335	The timestamp must be in RFC 3339 format (e.g. 2025-01-15T14:00:00Z).
   336	
   337	Examples:
   338	  zeroclaw cron add-at 2025-01-15T14:00:00Z 'Send reminder'
   339	  zeroclaw cron add-at 2025-12-31T23:59:00Z 'Happy New Year!'")]
   340	    AddAt {
   341	        /// One-shot timestamp in RFC3339 format
   342	        at: String,
   343	        /// Treat the argument as an agent prompt instead of a shell command
   344	        #[arg(long)]
   345	        agent: bool,
   346	        /// Restrict agent cron jobs to the specified tool names (repeatable, agent-only)

   209	#[test]
   210	fn gateway_path_prefix_accepts_valid_prefixes() {
   211	    for prefix in ["/zeroclaw", "/apps/zeroclaw", "/api/hassio_ingress/abc123"] {
   212	        let mut config = Config::default();
   213	        config.gateway.path_prefix = Some(prefix.into());
   214	        config
   215	            .validate()
   216	            .unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!("prefix {prefix:?} should be valid, got: {e}"));
   217	    }
   218	}
   219	
   220	#[test]
   221	fn gateway_path_prefix_rejects_unsafe_characters() {
   222	    for prefix in [
   223	        "/zero claw",
   224	        "/zero<claw",
   225	        "/zero>claw",
   226	        "/zero\"claw",
   227	        "/zero?query",
   228	        "/zero#frag",
   229	    ] {
   230	        let mut config = Config::default();
   231	        config.gateway.path_prefix = Some(prefix.into());
   232	        let err = config.validate().unwrap_err();
   233	        assert!(
   234	            err.to_string().contains("invalid character"),
   235	            "prefix {prefix:?} should be rejected, got: {err}"
   236	        );
   237	    }
   238	    // Leading/trailing whitespace is rejected by the starts_with('/') or
   239	    // invalid-character check — either way it must not pass validation.
   240	    for prefix in [" /zeroclaw ", " /zeroclaw"] {
   241	        let mut config = Config::default();
   242	        config.gateway.path_prefix = Some(prefix.into());
   243	        assert!(
   244	            config.validate().is_err(),
   245	            "whitespace-padded prefix {prefix:?} should be rejected"
   246	        );
   247	    }
   248	}

   426	fn config_memory_defaults_when_section_absent() {
   427	    let toml_str = "default_temperature = 0.7\n";
   428	    let parsed: Config = toml::from_str(toml_str).expect("minimal TOML should parse");
   429	    let mem = &parsed.memory;
   430	    assert!(!mem.backend.is_empty());
   431	    assert!(!mem.embedding_provider.is_empty());
   432	    let weight_sum = mem.vector_weight + mem.keyword_weight;

   436	    );

   463	#[test]
   464	fn config_toplevel_cli_section_with_whatsapp_parses() {
   465	    // Exact config from issue #3456
   466	    let toml_str = r#"
   467	[cli]
   468	
   469	[channels_config.whatsapp]
   470	session_path = "~/.zeroclaw/state/whatsapp-web/session.db"
   471	allowed_numbers = ["*"]
   472	"#;
   473	    let parsed: Config = toml::from_str(toml_str)
   474	        .expect("top-level [cli] section with [channels_config.whatsapp] should parse");
   475	    assert!(parsed.channels_config.whatsapp.is_some());
   476	    let wa = parsed.channels_config.whatsapp.unwrap();
   477	    assert_eq!(
   478	        wa.session_path.as_deref(),
   479	        Some("~/.zeroclaw/state/whatsapp-web/session.db")
   480	    );
   481	    assert_eq!(wa.allowed_numbers, vec!["*".to_string()]);
   482	}
   483	
   484	#[test]
   485	fn config_only_whatsapp_channel_parses() {

   489	allowed_numbers = ["*"]

   494	    assert!(
   495	        parsed.channels_config.cli,
   496	        "cli should default to true when omitted"
   497	    );
   498	}

  6160	    pub bluesky: Option<BlueskyConfig>,
  6161	    /// Voice call channel configuration (Twilio/Telnyx/Plivo).
  6162	    pub voice_call: Option<crate::channels::voice_call::VoiceCallConfig>,
  6163	    /// Voice wake word detection channel configuration.
  6164	    #[cfg(feature = "voice-wake")]
  6165	    pub voice_wake: Option<VoiceWakeConfig>,
  6166	    /// MQTT channel configuration (SOP listener).
  6167	    pub mqtt: Option<MqttConfig>,
  6168	    /// Base timeout in seconds for processing a single channel message (LLM + tools).
  6169	    /// Runtime uses this as a per-turn budget that scales with tool-loop depth
  6170	    /// (up to 4x, capped) so one slow/retried model call does not consume the
  6171	    /// entire conversation budget.
  6172	    /// Default: 300s for on-device LLMs (Ollama) which are slower than cloud APIs.
  6173	    #[serde(default = "default_channel_message_timeout_secs")]
  6174	    pub message_timeout_secs: u64,
  6175	    /// Whether to add acknowledgement reactions (👀 on receipt, ✅/⚠️ on
  6176	    /// completion) to incoming channel messages. Default: `true`.
  6177	    #[serde(default = "default_true")]
  6178	    pub ack_reactions: bool,
  6179	    /// Whether to send tool-call notification messages (e.g. `🔧 web_search_tool: …`)
  6180	    /// to channel users. When `false`, tool calls are still logged server-side but
  6181	    /// not forwarded as individual channel messages. Default: `false`.
  6182	    #[serde(default = "default_false")]
  6183	    pub show_tool_calls: bool,
  6184	    /// Persist channel conversation history to JSONL files so sessions survive
  6185	    /// daemon restarts. Files are stored in `{workspace}/sessions/`. Default: `true`.
  6186	    #[serde(default = "default_true")]
  6187	    pub session_persistence: bool,
  6188	    /// Session persistence backend: `"jsonl"` (legacy) or `"sqlite"` (new default).
  6189	    /// SQLite provides FTS5 search, metadata tracking, and TTL cleanup.
  6190	    #[serde(default = "default_session_backend")]
  6191	    pub session_backend: String,
  6192	    /// Auto-archive stale sessions older than this many hours. `0` disables. Default: `0`.
  6193	    #[serde(default)]
  6194	    pub session_ttl_hours: u32,
  6195	    /// Inbound message debounce window in milliseconds. When a sender fires
  6196	    /// multiple messages within this window, they are accumulated and dispatched
  6197	    /// as a single concatenated message. `0` disables debouncing. Default: `0`.
  6198	    #[serde(default)]
  6199	    pub debounce_ms: u64,
  6200	}

  6444	/// Discord bot channel configuration.
  6445	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema)]
  6446	pub struct DiscordConfig {
  6447	    /// Discord bot token (from Discord Developer Portal).
  6448	    pub bot_token: String,
  6449	    /// Optional guild (server) ID to restrict the bot to a single guild.
  6450	    pub guild_id: Option<String>,
  6451	    /// Allowed Discord user IDs. Empty = deny all.
  6452	    #[serde(default)]
  6453	    pub allowed_users: Vec<String>,
  6454	    /// When true, process messages from other bots (not just humans).
  6455	    /// The bot still ignores its own messages to prevent feedback loops.
  6456	    #[serde(default)]
  6457	    pub listen_to_bots: bool,
  6458	    /// When true, a newer Discord message from the same sender in the same channel
  6459	    /// cancels the in-flight request and starts a fresh response with preserved history.
  6460	    #[serde(default)]
  6461	    pub interrupt_on_new_message: bool,
  6462	    /// When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot.
  6463	    /// Other messages in the guild are silently ignored.
  6464	    #[serde(default)]
  6465	    pub mention_only: bool,
  6466	    /// Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
  6467	    /// Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.
  6468	    #[serde(default)]
  6469	    pub proxy_url: Option<String>,
  6470	    /// Streaming mode for progressive response delivery.
  6471	    /// `off` (default): single message. `partial`: editable draft updates.
  6472	    /// `multi_message`: split response into separate messages at paragraph boundaries.
  6473	    #[serde(default)]
  6474	    pub stream_mode: StreamMode,
  6475	    /// Minimum interval (ms) between draft message edits to avoid rate limits.
  6476	    /// Only used when `stream_mode = "partial"`.
  6477	    #[serde(default = "default_draft_update_interval_ms")]
  6478	    pub draft_update_interval_ms: u64,
  6479	    /// Delay (ms) between sending each message chunk in multi-message mode.
  6480	    /// Only used when `stream_mode = "multi_message"`.
  6481	    #[serde(default = "default_multi_message_delay_ms")]
  6482	    pub multi_message_delay_ms: u64,
  6483	    /// Stall-watchdog timeout in seconds. When non-zero, the bot will abort
  6484	    /// and retry if no progress is made within this duration. 0 = disabled.
  6485	    #[serde(default)]
  6486	    pub stall_timeout_secs: u64,
  6487	}

2026-05-31 12:29:51,103 - DEBUG - swea-tools-zeroclaw-labs__zeroclaw-5165 - Retrieved state from environment: {'working_dir': '/testbed'}

OBSERVATION:

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema)]
pub struct TelegramConfig {
    /// Telegram Bot API token (from @BotFather).
    pub bot_token: String,
    /// Allowed Telegram user IDs or usernames. Empty = deny all.
    pub allowed_users: Vec<String>,
    /// Streaming mode for progressive response delivery via message edits.
    #[serde(default)]
    pub stream_mode: StreamMode,
    /// Minimum interval (ms) between draft message edits to avoid rate limits.
    #[serde(default = "default_draft_update_interval_ms")]
    pub draft_update_interval_ms: u64,
    /// When true, a newer Telegram message from the same sender in the same chat
    /// cancels the in-flight request and starts a fresh response with preserved history.
    #[serde(default)]
    pub interrupt_on_new_message: bool,
    /// When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot in groups.
    /// Direct messages are always processed.
    #[serde(default)]
    pub mention_only: bool,
    /// Override for the top-level `ack_reactions` setting. When `None`, the
    /// channel falls back to `[channels_config].ack_reactions`. When set
    /// explicitly, it takes precedence.
    #[serde(default)]
    pub ack_reactions: Option<bool>,
    /// Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
    /// Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.
    #[serde(default)]
    pub proxy_url: Option<String>,
}

impl ChannelConfig for TelegramConfig {

> - Generates `prop_fields()`, `get_prop()`, `set_prop()`, `prop_is_secret()`, `init_defaults()`, `secret_fields()`, `encrypt_secrets()`, `decrypt_secrets()`
> - `get_prop`/`set_prop` use serde roundtrip — macro generates only metadata, not per-field type dispatch
> - `HasPropKind` trait maps types to `PropKind` at compile time; scalars in `traits.rs`, config enums via `impl_enum_prop_kind!`
> - Handles `HashMap<String, T>` with `#[nested]` and `Vec<String>` with `#[secret]`
> - All ~80 config structs annotated — every property individually addressable

> ### `zeroclaw props` CLI
> - `props list [--filter PREFIX] [--secrets]` — enumerate all properties with values, types, categories
> - `props get <path>` — get a value (secrets show set/unset status only)
> - `props set <path> [value]` — set a property; secrets auto-detect masked input; enums offer interactive `dialoguer::Select`
> - `props init [section]` — instantiate unconfigured sections with defaults (`enabled=false`)
> - `props complete [partial]` — dynamic shell tab-completion for property paths

> ### Additional changes
> - `enabled: bool` field on all channel configs for explicit activation
> - `backfill_enabled()` auto-enables channels present in config.toml without explicit `enabled` key
> - Shell completion hooks (bash/zsh/fish) wrap clap's generated function
> - Manual encrypt/decrypt helpers replaced by macro-derived methods
> - Exhaustive walk tests verify every prop and every enum variant

   167	    pub fn with_transcription(mut self, config: crate::config::TranscriptionConfig) -> Self {
   168	        if !config.enabled {
   169	            return self;
   170	        }
   171	        match super::transcription::TranscriptionManager::new(&config) {
   172	            Ok(m) => {
   173	                self.transcription_manager = Some(std::sync::Arc::new(m));
   174	                self.transcription = Some(config);
   175	            }
   176	            Err(e) => {
   177	                tracing::warn!(
   178	                    "transcription manager init failed, voice transcription disabled: {e}"
   179	                );
   180	            }
   181	        }
   182	        self
   183	    }
   184	
   185	    /// Configure text-to-speech for outgoing voice replies.
   186	    #[cfg(feature = "whatsapp-web")]
   187	    pub fn with_tts(mut self, config: crate::config::TtsConfig) -> Self {
   188	        if config.enabled {
   189	            self.tts_config = Some(config);
   190	        }
   191	        self
   192	    }
   193	
   194	    /// Set mention patterns for DM mention gating.
   195	    /// Each pattern string is compiled as a case-insensitive regex.

     1	# AGENTS.md — ZeroClaw
     2	
     3	Cross-tool agent instructions for any AI coding assistant working on this repository.
     4	
     5	## Commands
     6	
     7	```bash
     8	cargo fmt --all -- --check
     9	cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
    10	cargo test
    11	```
    12	
    13	Full pre-PR validation (recommended):
    14	
    15	```bash
    16	./dev/ci.sh all
    17	```
    18	
    19	Docs-only changes: run markdown lint and link-integrity checks. If touching bootstrap scripts: `bash -n install.sh`.
    20	
    21	## Project Snapshot
    22	
    23	ZeroClaw is a Rust-first autonomous agent runtime optimized for performance, efficiency, stability, extensibility, sustainability, and security.
    24	
    25	Core architecture is trait-driven and modular. Extend by implementing traits and registering in factory modules.
    26	
    27	Key extension points:
    28	
    29	- `src/providers/traits.rs` (`Provider`)
    30	- `src/channels/traits.rs` (`Channel`)
    31	- `src/tools/traits.rs` (`Tool`)
    32	- `src/memory/traits.rs` (`Memory`)
    33	- `src/observability/traits.rs` (`Observer`)
    34	- `src/runtime/traits.rs` (`RuntimeAdapter`)
    35	- `src/peripherals/traits.rs` (`Peripheral`) — hardware boards (STM32, RPi GPIO)
    36	
    37	## Repository Map
    38	
    39	- `src/main.rs` — CLI entrypoint and command routing
    40	- `src/lib.rs` — module exports and shared command enums
    41	- `src/config/` — schema + config loading/merging
    42	- `src/agent/` — orchestration loop
    43	- `src/gateway/` — webhook/gateway server
    44	- `src/security/` — policy, pairing, secret store
    45	- `src/memory/` — markdown/sqlite memory backends + embeddings/vector merge
    46	- `src/providers/` — model providers and resilient wrapper
    47	- `src/channels/` — Telegram/Discord/Slack/etc channels
    48	- `src/tools/` — tool execution surface (shell, file, memory, browser)
    49	- `src/peripherals/` — hardware peripherals (STM32, RPi GPIO)
    50	- `src/runtime/` — runtime adapters (currently native)
    51	- `docs/` — topic-based documentation (setup-guides, reference, ops, security, hardware, contributing, maintainers)
    52	- `.github/` — CI, templates, automation workflows
    53	
    54	## Risk Tiers
    55	
    56	- **Low risk**: docs/chore/tests-only changes
    57	- **Medium risk**: most `src/**` behavior changes without boundary/security impact
    58	- **High risk**: `src/security/**`, `src/runtime/**`, `src/gateway/**`, `src/tools/**`, `.github/workflows/**`, access-control boundaries
    59	
    60	When uncertain, classify as higher risk.
    61	
    62	## Workflow
    63	
    64	1. **Read before write** — inspect existing module, factory wiring, and adjacent tests before editing.
    65	2. **One concern per PR** — avoid mixed feature+refactor+infra patches.
    66	3. **Implement minimal patch** — no speculative abstractions, no config keys without a concrete use case.
    67	4. **Validate by risk tier** — docs-only: lightweight checks. Code changes: full relevant checks.
    68	5. **Document impact** — update PR notes for behavior, risk, side effects, and rollback.
    69	6. **Queue hygiene** — stacked PR: declare `Depends on #...`. Replacing old PR: declare `Supersedes #...`.
    70	
    71	Branch/commit/PR rules:
    72	- Work from a non-`master` branch. Open a PR to `master`; do not push directly.
    73	- Use conventional commit titles. Prefer small PRs (`size: XS/S/M`).
    74	- Follow `.github/pull_request_template.md` fully.
    75	- Never commit secrets, personal data, or real identity information (see `@docs/contributing/pr-discipline.md`).
    76	
    77	## Anti-Patterns
    78	
    79	- Do not add heavy dependencies for minor convenience.
    80	- Do not silently weaken security policy or access constraints.
    81	- Do not add speculative config/feature flags "just in case".
    82	- Do not mix massive formatting-only changes with functional changes.
    83	- Do not modify unrelated modules "while here".
    84	- Do not bypass failing checks without explicit explanation.
    85	- Do not hide behavior-changing side effects in refactor commits.
    86	- Do not include personal identity or sensitive information in test data, examples, docs, or commits.
    87	
    88	## Linked References
    89	
    90	- `@docs/contributing/change-playbooks.md` — adding providers, channels, tools, peripherals; security/gateway changes; architecture boundaries
    91	- `@docs/contributing/pr-discipline.md` — privacy rules, superseded-PR attribution/templates, handoff template
    92	- `@docs/contributing/docs-contract.md` — docs system contract, i18n rules, locale parity
    93

    11	/// Wait for shutdown signal (SIGINT or SIGTERM).
    12	/// SIGHUP is explicitly ignored so the daemon survives terminal/SSH disconnects.
    13	async fn wait_for_shutdown_signal() -> Result<()> {
    14	    #[cfg(unix)]
    15	    {
    16	        use tokio::signal::unix::{SignalKind, signal};
    17	
    18	        let mut sigint = signal(SignalKind::interrupt())?;
    19	        let mut sigterm = signal(SignalKind::terminate())?;
    20	        let mut sighup = signal(SignalKind::hangup())?;
    21	
    22	        loop {
    23	            tokio::select! {
    24	                _ = sigint.recv() => {
    25	                    tracing::info!("Received SIGINT, shutting down...");
    26	                    break;
    27	                }
    28	                _ = sigterm.recv() => {
    29	                    tracing::info!("Received SIGTERM, shutting down...");
    30	                    break;
    31	                }
    32	                _ = sighup.recv() => {
    33	                    tracing::info!("Received SIGHUP, ignoring (daemon stays running)");
    34	                }
    35	            }
    36	        }
    37	    }
    38	
    39	    #[cfg(not(unix))]
    40	    {
    41	        tokio::signal::ctrl_c().await?;
    42	        tracing::info!("Received Ctrl+C, shutting down...");
    43	    }
    44	
    45	    Ok(())
    46	}

    48	pub async fn run(config: Config, host: String, port: u16) -> Result<()> {
    49	    let initial_backoff = config.reliability.channel_initial_backoff_secs.max(1);
    50	    let max_backoff = config
    51	        .reliability
    52	        .channel_max_backoff_secs
    53	        .max(initial_backoff);
    54	
    55	    crate::health::mark_component_ok("daemon");
    56	
    57	    // Shared broadcast channel so all daemon components (gateway, cron,
    58	    // heartbeat) can publish real-time events to dashboard clients.
    59	    let (event_tx, _rx) = tokio::sync::broadcast::channel::<serde_json::Value>(256);
    60	
    61	    if config.heartbeat.enabled {
    62	        let _ =
    63	            crate::heartbeat::engine::HeartbeatEngine::ensure_heartbeat_file(&config.workspace_dir)
    64	                .await;
    65	    }
    66	
    67	    let mut handles: Vec<JoinHandle<()>> = vec![spawn_state_writer(config.clone())];
    68	
    69	    {
    70	        let gateway_cfg = config.clone();
    71	        let gateway_host = host.clone();
    72	        let gateway_event_tx = event_tx.clone();
    73	        handles.push(spawn_component_supervisor(
    74	            "gateway",
    75	            initial_backoff,
    76	            max_backoff,
    77	            move || {
    78	                let cfg = gateway_cfg.clone();
    79	                let host = gateway_host.clone();
    80	                let tx = gateway_event_tx.clone();
    81	                async move {
    82	                    Box::pin(crate::gateway::run_gateway(&host, port, cfg, Some(tx))).await
    83	                }
    84	            },
    85	        ));
    86	    }
    87	
    88	    {
    89	        if has_supervised_channels(&config) {
    90	            let channels_cfg = config.clone();
    91	            handles.push(spawn_component_supervisor(
    92	                "channels",
    93	                initial_backoff,
    94	                max_backoff,
    95	                move || {
    96	                    let cfg = channels_cfg.clone();
    97	                    async move { Box::pin(crate::channels::start_channels(cfg)).await }
    98	                },
    99	            ));
   100	        } else {
   101	            crate::health::mark_component_ok("channels");
   102	            tracing::info!("No real-time channels configured; channel supervisor disabled");
   103	        }
   104	    }
   105	
   106	    // Wire up MQTT SOP listener if configured
   107	    if let Some(ref mqtt_config) = config.channels_config.mqtt {
   108	        let mqtt_cfg = mqtt_config.clone();
   109	        handles.push(spawn_component_supervisor(
   110	            "mqtt",
   111	            initial_backoff,
   112	            max_backoff,
   113	            move || {
   114	                let cfg = mqtt_cfg.clone();
   115	                async move { Box::pin(run_mqtt_sop_listener(&cfg)).await }
   116	            },
   117	        ));
   118	    } else {
   119	        crate::health::mark_component_ok("mqtt");
   120	    }
   121	
   122	    if config.heartbeat.enabled {
   123	        let heartbeat_cfg = config.clone();
   124	        handles.push(spawn_component_supervisor(
   125	            "heartbeat",
   126	            initial_backoff,

   131	            },
   132	        ));
   133	    }

   136	        let scheduler_cfg = config.clone();

   138	        handles.push(spawn_component_supervisor(

   162	    wait_for_shutdown_signal().await?;
   163	    crate::health::mark_component_error("daemon", "shutdown requested");
   164	
   165	    for handle in &handles {
   166	        handle.abort();
   167	    }
   168	    for handle in handles {
   169	        let _ = handle.await;
   170	    }
   171	
   172	    Ok(())
   173	}
   174	
   175	pub fn state_file_path(config: &Config) -> PathBuf {
   176	    config
   177	        .config_path
   178	        .parent()
   179	        .map_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("."), PathBuf::from)
   180	        .join("daemon_state.json")
   181	}
   182	
   183	fn spawn_state_writer(config: Config) -> JoinHandle<()> {
   184	    tokio::spawn(async move {
   185	        let path = state_file_path(&config);
   186	        if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {
   187	            let _ = tokio::fs::create_dir_all(parent).await;
   188	        }
   189	
   190	        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(STATUS_FLUSH_SECONDS));
   191	        loop {
   192	            interval.tick().await;
   193	            let mut json = crate::health::snapshot_json();
   194	            if let Some(obj) = json.as_object_mut() {
   195	                obj.insert(
   196	                    "written_at".into(),
   197	                    serde_json::json!(Utc::now().to_rfc3339()),
   198	                );
   199	            }
   200	            let data = serde_json::to_vec_pretty(&json).unwrap_or_else(|_| b"{}".to_vec());
   201	            let _ = tokio::fs::write(&path, data).await;
   202	        }
   203	    })
   204	}
   205	
   206	fn spawn_component_supervisor<F, Fut>(
   207	    name: &'static str,
   208	    initial_backoff_secs: u64,
   209	    max_backoff_secs: u64,
   210	    mut run_component: F,
   211	) -> JoinHandle<()>
   212	where
   213	    F: FnMut() -> Fut + Send + 'static,
   214	    Fut: Future<Output = Result<()>> + Send + 'static,
   215	{
   216	    tokio::spawn(async move {
   217	        let mut backoff = initial_backoff_secs.max(1);
   218	        let max_backoff = max_backoff_secs.max(backoff);
   219	
   220	        loop {
   221	            crate::health::mark_component_ok(name);
   222	            match run_component().await {
   223	                Ok(()) => {
   224	                    crate::health::mark_component_error(name, "component exited unexpectedly");
   225	                    tracing::warn!("Daemon component '{name}' exited unexpectedly");
   226	                    // Clean exit — reset backoff since the component ran successfully
   227	                    backoff = initial_backoff_secs.max(1);
   228	                }
   229	                Err(e) => {
   230	                    crate::health::mark_component_error(name, e.to_string());
   231	                    tracing::error!("Daemon component '{name}' failed: {e}");
   232	                }
   233	            }
   234	
   235	            crate::health::bump_component_restart(name);
   236	            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(backoff)).await;
   237	            // Double backoff AFTER sleeping so first error uses initial_backoff
   238	            backoff = backoff.saturating_mul(2).min(max_backoff);
   239	        }
   240	    })
   241	}
   242	
   243	async fn run_heartbeat_worker(config: Config) -> Result<()> {
   244	    use crate::heartbeat::engine::{
   245	        HeartbeatEngine, HeartbeatTask, TaskPriority, TaskStatus, compute_adaptive_interval,
   246	    };
   247	    use std::sync::Arc;
   248	
   249	    let observer: std::sync::Arc<dyn crate::observability::Observer> =
   250	        std::sync::Arc::from(crate::observability::create_observer(&config.observability));
   251	    let engine = HeartbeatEngine::new(
   252	        config.heartbeat.clone(),
   253	        config.workspace_dir.clone(),
   254	        observer,
   255	    );
   256	    let metrics = engine.metrics();
   257	    let delivery = resolve_heartbeat_delivery(&config)?;
   258	    let two_phase = config.heartbeat.two_phase;
   259	    let adaptive = config.heartbeat.adaptive;
   260	    let start_time = std::time::Instant::now();
   261	
   262	    // ── Deadman watcher ──────────────────────────────────────────
   263	    let deadman_timeout = config.heartbeat.deadman_timeout_minutes;
   264	    if deadman_timeout > 0 {
   265	        let dm_metrics = Arc::clone(&metrics);
   266	        let dm_config = config.clone();
   267	        let dm_delivery = delivery.clone();
   268	        tokio::spawn(async move {
   269	            let check_interval = Duration::from_secs(60);
   270	            let timeout = chrono::Duration::minutes(i64::from(deadman_timeout));
   271	            loop {
   272	                tokio::time::sleep(check_interval).await;
   273	                let last_tick = dm_metrics.lock().last_tick_at;
   274	                if let Some(last) = last_tick {
   275	                    if chrono::Utc::now() - last > timeout {
   276	                        let alert = format!(
   277	                            "⚠️ Heartbeat dead-man's switch: no tick in {deadman_timeout} minutes"
   278	                        );
   279	                        let (channel, target) =
   280	                            if let Some(ch) = &dm_config.heartbeat.deadman_channel {
   281	                                let to = dm_config
   282	                                    .heartbeat
   283	                                    .deadman_to
   284	                                    .as_deref()
   285	                                    .or(dm_config.heartbeat.to.as_deref())
   286	                                    .unwrap_or_default();
   287	                                (ch.clone(), to.to_string())
   288	                            } else if let Some((ch, to)) = &dm_delivery {
   289	                                (ch.clone(), to.clone())
   290	                            } else {
   291	                                continue;
   292	                            };
   293	                        let delivery_fut = crate::cron::scheduler::deliver_announcement(
   294	                            &dm_config, &channel, &target, &alert,
   295	                        );
   296	                        match tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(30), delivery_fut).await {
   297	                            Ok(Err(e)) => {
   298	                                tracing::warn!("Deadman alert delivery failed: {e}");
   299	                            }
   300	                            Err(_) => {
   301	                                tracing::warn!("Deadman alert delivery timed out (30s)");
   302	                            }
   303	                            Ok(Ok(())) => {}
   304	                        }
   305	                    }
   306	                }
   307	            }
   308	        });
   309	    }
   310	
   311	    let base_interval = config.heartbeat.interval_minutes.max(1);
   312	    let mut sleep_mins = base_interval;
   313	
   314	    loop {
   315	        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(u64::from(sleep_mins) * 60)).await;
   316	
   317	        // Update uptime
   318	        {
   319	            let mut m = metrics.lock();
   320	            m.uptime_secs = start_time.elapsed().as_secs();
   321	        }
   322	
   323	        let tick_start = std::time::Instant::now();
   324	
   325	        // Collect runnable tasks (active only, sorted by priority)
   326	        let mut tasks = engine.collect_runnable_tasks().await?;
   327	        let has_high_priority = tasks.iter().any(|t| t.priority == TaskPriority::High);
   328	
   329	        if tasks.is_empty() {
   330	            if let Some(fallback) = config
   331	                .heartbeat
   332	                .message
   333	                .as_deref()
   334	                .map(str::trim)
   335	                .filter(|m| !m.is_empty())
   336	            {
   337	                tasks.push(HeartbeatTask {
   338	                    text: fallback.to_string(),
   339	                    priority: TaskPriority::Medium,
   340	                    status: TaskStatus::Active,
   341	                });
   342	            } else {
   343	                #[allow(clippy::cast_precision_loss)]
   344	                let elapsed = tick_start.elapsed().as_millis() as f64;
   345	                metrics.lock().record_success(elapsed);
   346	                continue;
   347	            }
   348	        }
   349	
   350	        // ── Phase 1: LLM decision (two-phase mode) ──────────────
   351	        let tasks_to_run = if two_phase {
   352	            let decision_prompt = format!(
   353	                "[Heartbeat Task | decision] {}",
   354	                HeartbeatEngine::build_decision_prompt(&tasks),
   355	            );
   356	            let phase1_fut = Box::pin(crate::agent::run(
   357	                config.clone(),
   358	                Some(decision_prompt),
   359	                None,
   360	                None,
   361	                0.0,
   362	                vec![],
   363	                false,
   364	                None,
   365	                None,
   366	            ));
   367	            let phase1_result = if config.heartbeat.task_timeout_secs > 0 {
   368	                match tokio::time::timeout(
   369	                    Duration::from_secs(config.heartbeat.task_timeout_secs),
   370	                    phase1_fut,
   371	                )
   372	                .await
   373	                {
   374	                    Ok(r) => r,
   375	                    Err(_) => Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
   376	                        "Phase 1 decision timed out ({}s)",
   377	                        config.heartbeat.task_timeout_secs
   378	                    )),
   379	                }
   380	            } else {
   381	                phase1_fut.await
   382	            };
   383	            match phase1_result {
   384	                Ok(response) => {
   385	                    let indices = HeartbeatEngine::parse_decision_response(&response, tasks.len());
   386	                    if indices.is_empty() {
   387	                        tracing::info!("💓 Heartbeat Phase 1: skip (nothing to do)");
   388	                        crate::health::mark_component_ok("heartbeat");
   389	                        #[allow(clippy::cast_precision_loss)]
   390	                        let elapsed = tick_start.elapsed().as_millis() as f64;
   391	                        metrics.lock().record_success(elapsed);
   392	                        continue;
   393	                    }
   394	                    tracing::info!(
   395	                        "💓 Heartbeat Phase 1: run {} of {} tasks",
   396	                        indices.len(),
   397	                        tasks.len()
   398	                    );
   399	                    indices
   400	                        .into_iter()
   401	                        .filter_map(|i| tasks.get(i).cloned())
   402	                        .collect()
   403	                }
   404	                Err(e) => {
   405	                    tracing::warn!("💓 Heartbeat Phase 1 failed, running all tasks: {e}");
   406	                    tasks
   407	                }
   408	            }
   409	        } else {
   410	            tasks
   411	        };
   412	
   413	        // ── Phase 2: Execute selected tasks ─────────────────────
   414	        // Re-read session context on every tick so we pick up messages
   415	        // that arrived since the daemon started.
   416	        let session_context = if config.heartbeat.load_session_context {
   417	            load_heartbeat_session_context(&config)
   418	        } else {
   419	            None
   420	        };
   421	
   422	        // Create memory once per tick for recall + consolidation.
   423	        let heartbeat_memory: Option<Box<dyn crate::memory::Memory>> =
   424	            crate::memory::create_memory(
   425	                &config.memory,
   426	                &config.workspace_dir,
   427	                config.api_key.as_deref(),
   428	            )
   429	            .ok();
   430	
   431	        let mut tick_had_error = false;
   432	        for task in &tasks_to_run {
   433	            let task_start = std::time::Instant::now();
   434	            let task_prompt = format!("[Heartbeat Task | {}] {}", task.priority, task.text);
   435	
   436	            // Recall relevant memories so heartbeat tasks have context awareness.
   437	            // Exclude `Conversation` memories to prevent chat context from
   438	       <response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

   905	async fn run_mqtt_sop_listener(config: &crate::config::MqttConfig) -> Result<()> {
   906	    use crate::config::SopConfig;
   907	    use crate::memory::NoneMemory;
   908	    use crate::sop::{SopAuditLogger, SopEngine};
   909	    use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
   910	
   911	    // Initialize SOP engine
   912	    let engine = Arc::new(Mutex::new(SopEngine::new(SopConfig::default())));
   913	
   914	    // Initialize SOP audit logger with NoneMemory (MQTT listener is headless)
   915	    let audit = Arc::new(SopAuditLogger::new(Arc::new(NoneMemory)));
   916	
   917	    // Validate MQTT config and run the listener
   918	    config.validate()?;
   919	    crate::channels::mqtt::run_mqtt_sop_listener(config, engine, audit).await
   920	}

   946	    #[tokio::test]
   947	    async fn supervisor_marks_error_and_restart_on_failure() {
   948	        let handle = spawn_component_supervisor("daemon-test-fail", 1, 1, || async {
   949	            anyhow::bail!("boom")
   950	        });
   951	
   952	        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;
   953	        handle.abort();
   954	        let _ = handle.await;
   955	
   956	        let snapshot = crate::health::snapshot_json();
   957	        let component = &snapshot["components"]["daemon-test-fail"];
   958	        assert_eq!(component["status"], "error");
   959	        assert!(component["restart_count"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0) >= 1);
   960	        assert!(
   961	            component["last_error"]
   962	                .as_str()
   963	                .unwrap_or("")
   964	                .contains("boom")
   965	        );
   966	    }
   967	
   968	    #[tokio::test]
   969	    async fn supervisor_marks_unexpected_exit_as_error() {
   970	        let handle = spawn_component_supervisor("daemon-test-exit", 1, 1, || async { Ok(()) });
   971	
   972	        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;
   973	        handle.abort();
   974	        let _ = handle.await;
   975	
   976	        let snapshot = crate::health::snapshot_json();
   977	        let component = &snapshot["components"]["daemon-test-exit"];
   978	        assert_eq!(component["status"], "error");
   979	        assert!(component["restart_count"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0) >= 1);

   981	            component["last_error"]
